topic.title: Assignment 5 FAQs: Common Confusions Clarified
topic.text:
Q1: Do I need to build a completely new workflow for A5 with just 3 nodes (webhook → LLM → response)?

A: No. You're not building a new workflow from scratch. Your A5 Gradio/Streamlit app should connect to your existing A4 workflow via webhook. The webhook replaces your manual trigger, but you should still include the main requirements from A3 and A4 (data fetching, processing, LLM analysis). You're essentially adding Gradio/Streamlit as a user interface layer on top of your A4 workflow, not replacing it entirely.

Q2: Should users need to know my data schema to use the Gradio app?

A: No. Design your interface so it's intuitive. Either use clear input fields with labels that guide users on what to enter, or make your LLM prompts flexible enough to handle free-form text. The user shouldn't need to understand your backend schema. That's the whole point of building a good interface.

Q3: Should I enable "Continue on Error" on all nodes or just specific ones?

A: Use "Continue on Error" strategically for nodes that might fail due to external factors beyond your control (API downtime, rate limits, network issues, missing files). Examples include:

  • HTTP/API requests
  • RSS feeds
  • File read operations
  • External service calls

Do NOT use it on your core workflow logic nodes. If those fail, it usually indicates a real problem in your workflow that needs to be fixed, not bypassed. Suppressing errors on internal logic can hide bugs and make debugging much harder.

Q4: Can my Gradio app just simulate or document my A4 logic instead of actually running it?

A: No. Your Gradio interface must actually run your A4 logic and produce real results. The assignment requires "Connects to your A4 workflow (or runs it directly)". This means when a user inputs data, they should get actual results from your Madison logic, not a simulation or description. Connect to your n8n workflow via webhook (you can use webhook authentication/API keys for security). The interface must be functional, not just documentation.

Q5: How do I handle the public deployment requirement if my n8n workflow is running locally?

A: If your Gradio/Streamlit app is deployed on HuggingFace/Streamlit Cloud but your n8n workflow is on your local computer, they can't communicate directly. You have three options:

Option 1: Temporary URL (ngrok) - Free

  • Use a tool like ngrok to create a temporary public URL for your local n8n
  • Submit a 2-3 minute demo video showing your application working end-to-end (since the URL won't be live during grading)
  • Note: This URL isn't permanent and can't be used in your portfolio

Option 2: Deploy n8n to a cloud platform - Free/Low-cost

  • Railway.app (~$5 for the month) or Render.com (free tier)
  • Submit your permanent URL in your PDF as originally planned
  • Portfolio-ready solution
  • Note: Requires some technical setup (Docker, environment variables)

Option 3: n8n Cloud - $20/month

  • Easiest deployment option - works immediately
  • Submit your permanent URL in your PDF as originally planned
  • Cancel after grading if you don't need it long-term
  • Portfolio-ready solution

For Options 2 & 3, submit your permanent URL in the PDF and I can test it anytime. For Option 1, include your demo video link in the PDF AND as a comment on canvas. Most students have successfully used Option 1 for their user testing by keeping their applications running during testing sessions.

Note: I'll keep updating this FAQ as more common questions come up, so check back whenever you see the announcement has been modified.
